name: Giovanni Hidalgo
description: Puerto Rican percussionist and music educator
abstract: Giovanni Hidalgo, also known as "Mañenguito" (born November 22, 1963, in San Juan, Puerto Rico), is a Grammy Award-winning Latin jazz percussionist, widely regarded as one of the greatest conga players in the world. Renowned for his virtuosic speed, precision, and innovation, he revolutionized hand drumming by blending Afro-Caribbean rhy…

about_quotes.speaker: Don Alias
about_quotes.evidence: I asked for ... Giovanni Hidalgo ... So now I’ve got a band with the Brecker brothers, four of the greatest percussionists in the world
about_quotes.doc_title: Don Alias interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 6, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: I had Luisito Quintero and Giovanni Hidalgo, the master congero to the mix. We rehearsed and we played with that group in Montreal.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Louie Bellson
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee mentions 'Dr. Giovanni' as a pickpocket on the bill at Florentine Gardens in the 1930s, but the interviewer's mention of 'Giovanni Hidalgo' is a separate, later context involving Latin percussionists. The interviewee does not confirm knowing Giovanni Hidalgo personally, only listing
about_quotes.doc_title: Louie Bellson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: David Finck
about_quotes.evidence: It sounded like a Giovanni Hidalgo conga solo to me. ... He’s great. He’s Puerto Rican and great, great conga player.
about_quotes.doc_title: David Finck interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, May 29, 2015

about_quotes.speaker: Marty Sheller
about_quotes.evidence: one of the sessions that I mentioned to you where I wrote some music for Giovanni Hidalgo
about_quotes.doc_title: Marty Sheller interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 9, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Slide Hampton
about_quotes.evidence: I remember Giovanni Hidalgo would do for us. He would show us different rhythms.
about_quotes.doc_title: Slide Hampton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
